question.text: A Composite Insurance Agent can represent a maximum of how many insurers in total across different categories?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A Composite Insurance Agent can represent a maximum of 1 life insurer + 1 general insurer + 1 health insurer + 1 mono-line insurer, totalling 4 insurers. This is different from a Corporate Agent (composite) which can represent up to 3 life + 3 general + 3 health insurers. **The Trap:** 9 insurers (3+3+3) is the limit for a Corporate Agent (composite), not a Composite Insurance Agent. Candidates frequently confuse these two categories. 3 insurers (1+1+1) misses the mono-line insurer category. **Cross-Exam Context:** Insurance intermediary regulations are tested in NISM Series X-B and also in IRDA licensing exams. Understanding the distinction between agent types is critical for compliance. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When advising clients, understand that a Composite Insurance Agent has limited choice (4 companies), while a Corporate Agent or Direct Broker offers wider product comparison. This impacts the breadth of advice available.
